from_session: MPI-306 mock-up phase — three mock-ups, C2 approved, build plan written

session.name: App carousel frame — design APPROVED, Phase 1 build not started

goal.original: Design the portable app UI frame (MPI-306), then build it.
goal.summary: Mock-up phase COMPLETE and user-approved. Mock-up C2 IS the design. A 3-phase build plan exists and is agreed. NOTHING IS BUILT — no app UI code was written. The next session runs PHASE 1 ONLY (the portable frame), which is code, not design.

completed: Three independent mock-ups via the `impeccable` skill (A workbench / B spine / C surgical). C chosen, revised to C2, approved verbatim: 'This is it. Very good. ... This is the design.'
Approved composition recorded in docs/playbooks/add-app/ui/carousel-frame.md § The approved composition — it OVERRIDES the older sketch in that file wherever they disagree.
Tier cost corrected from fabricated seconds to MEASURED relative percentages (user supplied a real A/B: 20-step raw 386s / 8-step 100s / 4-step 51s → baseline / ~25% / ~13%).
Declared step `fields` contract added to the design (one row under the canvas, frame renders it from a declaration).
pending: PHASE 1 — the portable frame (steps 1-6 of plan.md). THIS IS THE NEXT ACTION.
Phase 2 — Head Swap as the frame's proof (step 7).
Phase 3 — hold-until-Apply run-path change (step 8), deliberately a separate diff.
User: head-swap LoRA fp32-vs-fp16 A/B, then R2 upload (NOT authorised). Head Swap cannot RUN until this lands — the graph 404s on the LoRA.

completed: docs/playbooks/add-app/ui/carousel-frame.md — THE design record. § The approved composition holds the exact gradient values, the slot rule, the ticker-navigates reversal, the measured tier percentages, and the settled odds and ends. § Steps are DATA now also holds the declared-`fields` contract.
.agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/plan.md — the 3-phase build plan with per-step verify criteria.
.agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/validation.md — what the mock-up phase produced and verified, and an explicit 'nothing is built' note.
pending: docs/playbooks/add-app/existing-apps/head-swap.md still says 'NOT YET BUILT — design record only' and lists MPI-304 as a blocker. Both stale — refresh when the UI lands.
A memory entry for the mpi_box contract once the gizmo actually ships (deferred twice now; still deferred).

UNCARDED CLEANUP: `--lib-bg: oklch(0.26 0.020 350)` is declared LOCALLY in both MpiAppLibrary.css:34 and MpiModelManager.css:39 rather than globally. The app frame will be the third local copy — this is DELIBERATE (see carousel-frame.md), but worth a card if a fourth surface ever wants it.

next_action.description: Implement PHASE 1 ONLY of .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/plan.md — the portable, app-agnostic carousel frame. Read docs/playbooks/add-app/ui/carousel-frame.md § The approved composition FIRST; the design is settled and must NOT be re-derived or re-litigated. Open the reference artefact .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/research/mockup-C2.html to see the approved composition working (it is a throwaway scratchpad file, NOT code to copy wholesale — the real build follows repo conventions: ComponentFactory.create, qs/on from utils/dom, CSS vars, icons.js, BEM). Build the frame generically with NOTHING Head-Swap-specific in it; Head Swap is Phase 2's proof. Do NOT touch the run path (Phase 3) — the frame is built against today's enqueue-time gallery behaviour, which means the last step's Apply/Discard are inert until Phase 3. That is expected, not a bug.

key_decisions: SEQUENCING (user-decided): frame first generically, Head Swap as its proof — NOT Head-Swap-first-then-extract. Building Head Swap first would bake its shape into the frame, the exact trap `steps`-as-data exists to prevent.
SEQUENCING (user-decided): hold-until-Apply is a SEPARATE phase after the frame, so a UI regression and a data-path regression never land in the same diff.

The step ticker NAVIGATES. This OVERTURNS the earlier 'step dots indicate but do not navigate' decision. All three mock-ups independently rejected inert dots — a row that indicates but refuses clicks reads as disabled, not informational. Resolution: make it real.
Middle steps are the bounded centred canvas with title above and hint below — NO annotation column. Mock-up C's 60/40 split was rejected by the user.

The slot is a PLACEHOLDER, not a container: empty = bordered box + icon; FILLED = the image IS the box (width/height auto, no background, border hugging the image at its own aspect). No crop, no letterbox padding. This is the rule most likely to be got wrong.
The divider is INSET top and bottom (~62% height, fading at both ends) and is quiet --line, never an accent colour. It never touches the chrome.
Background is an ambient gradient CENTRE-OUT HORIZONTALLY, grounded on --lib-bg so the app frame and the two Libraries read as one family. Exact stops to be TUNED LIVE IN THE APP, not in a mock-up.
CSS tokens stay LOCAL to the component (deliberate — see carousel-frame.md); do not promote them to styles/01_base.css.

Tier cost labels are RELATIVE percentages, never absolute seconds — a hardcoded ETA is a lie on every GPU but the one it was measured on. Progress during a run must come from SERVER TRUTH (steps completed), never a baked per-tier duration.

A step may declare `fields` (one row between canvas and hint). The FRAME renders the row from the declaration so every gizmo's controls match for free. HARD CAP: one row, no nesting/panels/accordions — a gizmo wanting more means the step should SPLIT.
Mid-run navigation is ALLOWED (the run keeps going); only CLOSING with an unapplied result prompts to discard.
constraints: `steps` DOES NOT EXIST IN CODE. It is design only. The AppDef typedef (js/data/appsRegistry.js:22-37) has 9 properties and none is `steps`; the only occurrence of the word in that file is a comment on line 142. Phase 1 INTRODUCES it — it is not a half-finished thing to complete.

REUSE js/utils/cropTool.js — do NOT fork it. It is a standalone factory `createCropTool({overlayCanvas, targetElement, onChange})` (line 37) with 8 handles, body drag, clamping and ratio-lock already correct. Add a `showGrid` option (default true) so the head-swap box can omit the rule-of-thirds grid, which is drawn around line 446.
cropTool works in NORMALIZED [0..1]; the graph wants TOP-LEFT SOURCE PIXELS. Multiply by natural dimensions at inject time.

The box MUST CLAMP to image bounds. Mpi Box Crop returns the intersection and does NOT pad, so an off-edge box silently yields a non-square crop — a distorted head the user blames on the model. Clamp the EDGES before deriving w/h.
FIXED-PROMPT app: the graph has NO Input_Positive/Input_Negative — both prompts are BAKED. Never design or build a prompt box for Head Swap.
NO SEED UI, EVER — random seed every generation, never shown or saved.
Slot previews are a HARD REQUIREMENT (you cannot box what you cannot see) and are still an open MPI-259 item for video/audio; images are settled by the approved composition.

Standing UI law applies and is NOT restated in the design doc: ComponentFactory.create (never modify js/components/factory.js), BEM .mpi-block__element--modifier, qs/qsa/gid and on/off from js/utils/dom.js, CSS vars from styles/01_base.css, icons from js/utils/icons.js, Events.on/emit with stored unsubscribes, and a destroy() that cleans up everything. Register new CSS in js/shell/preloadStyles.js (MpiBaseApp.css is already listed at line 49) and document props in js/components/types.js.
State: never mutate sub-objects — replace the top-level key. App inputs live in state.s_appInputs[appId].
Shared tree: commit by explicit pathspec, never git add -A. A concurrent session owns MPI-288 and created MPI-305 — do not touch either.
DO NOT upload anything to R2 — still not authorised, pending the fp32-vs-fp16 A/B.
Head Swap CANNOT RUN regardless of this work (LoRA 404). The frame is buildable and inspectable without it; do not treat a failed generation as a frame bug.

rules_active: feedback: no seed UI; no toast on user Stop; error dialog vs toast

recent_events.event: MPI-306 moved todo -> doing (mock-up phase started)
mock-up phase complete; C2 approved by user as THE design
build plan written; tier % corrected to measured 25/13

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/901a6596-a56b-4b4e-a795-48704bcfc1a1.json, then read docs/playbooks/add-app/ui/carousel-frame.md § The approved composition and .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/plan.md. The design is SETTLED and user-approved — do not re-derive or re-litigate it. Build PHASE 1 ONLY: the portable, app-agnostic carousel frame (plan steps 1-6). Nothing Head-Swap-specific goes in the frame; Head Swap is Phase 2's proof. Do not touch the run path — that is Phase 3, deliberately a separate diff, which means the last step's Apply/Discard stay inert for now and that is expected. Key things that are easy to get wrong: a FILLED media slot is the image itself with a border hugging its own aspect (no box chrome, no crop, no letterbox padding); the divider is inset top and bottom and is quiet --line, never an accent; the step ticker NAVIGATES (this overturns the older 'dots do not navigate' rule); the box gizmo REUSES js/utils/cropTool.js via a new showGrid option rather than forking it, and must clamp EDGES to the image before deriving w/h; tier labels are relative percentages (baseline / ~25% / ~13%), never absolute seconds. Head Swap cannot RUN yet (its LoRA 404s pending an unauthorised R2 upload) — do not treat that as a frame bug. Reference artefact showing the approved composition working: .agents/mpi-kanban/tasks/MPI-306/research/mockup-C2.html.
